name: Submariner Date
description: The Rolex Submariner Date reference 126610LN is the current-generation of the world's most iconic dive watch. Introduced in 2020, it features the calibre 3235 movement with 70-hour power reserve, a 41mm Oystersteel case, and the signature black Cerachrom bezel. The Submariner was first introduced in 1953 and has since become the archetype against which all dive watches are measured.

name: GMT-Master II 'Pepsi'
description: The GMT-Master II 'Pepsi' reference 126710BLRO features the iconic blue and red Cerachrom bezel insert. Originally designed for Pan Am pilots to track multiple time zones, the GMT-Master II continues to be one of the most sought-after Rolex models. The current generation uses the calibre 3285 with a redesigned GMT mechanism.

name: Cosmograph Daytona
description: The Rolex Cosmograph Daytona is the ultimate racing chronograph. First introduced in 1963, the current reference 126500LN features the calibre 4131 chronograph movement, a Cerachrom bezel with tachymetre scale, and the classic Panda dial configuration. The Daytona remains one of the most collectible and coveted watches in the world.

name: Speedmaster Moonwatch Professional
description: The Omega Speedmaster Professional is the legendary 'Moonwatch' — the first watch worn on the Moon during the Apollo 11 mission on July 20, 1969. The current reference features the manually wound calibre 3861, a Master Chronometer-certified evolution of the historic calibre 321. With its Hesalite crystal and step dial, this model stays true to the original Moonwatch DNA.

name: Seamaster Diver 300M
description: The Omega Seamaster Diver 300M is one of the world's most recognizable dive watches, famously worn by James Bond since GoldenEye (1995). The current generation features the Master Chronometer calibre 8800, a ceramic wave-pattern dial, and a ceramic bezel with a liquidmetal diving scale. It combines professional dive watch capabilities with everyday elegance.

name: Nautilus
description: The Patek Philippe Nautilus reference 5711/1A is one of the most iconic and sought-after luxury sports watches ever made. Designed by Gérald Genta in 1976, the Nautilus features a distinctive porthole-shaped case with 'ears' on either side. The 5711 was discontinued in 2021, making it one of the most coveted references in modern watchmaking. Its clean blue dial, integrated bracelet, and ultra-thin profile defined a generation of luxury sports watches.

name: Royal Oak 'Jumbo'
description: The Audemars Piguet Royal Oak is the watch that created the luxury steel sports watch category. Designed by Gérald Genta in 1972, its octagonal bezel with exposed hexagonal screws, integrated bracelet, and 'Tapisserie' dial became instant icons. Reference 15500ST continues the legacy with the calibre 4302 automatic movement and maintains the original 39mm size.

name: Santos de Cartier Medium
description: The Cartier Santos is one of the most historically significant wristwatches ever created. In 1904, Louis Cartier designed it for Brazilian aviator Alberto Santos-Dumont, who needed a watch he could read while flying. The current Santos de Cartier features the QuickSwitch interchangeable bracelet/strap system and SmartLink micro-adjustment, combining over a century of heritage with modern innovation.

name: Tank Must
description: The Cartier Tank is one of the most iconic watch designs in history, inspired by the aerial view of a Renault tank during World War I. Created by Louis Cartier in 1917, the Tank's rectangular case with brancards (side bars) forming the lugs has influenced watch design for over a century. The Tank Must offers this timeless design at a more accessible price point.

name: Prospex 62MAS Reinterpretation
description: The Seiko SPB143 is a modern reinterpretation of the legendary 62MAS, Seiko's first professional dive watch from 1965. Featuring the 6R35 automatic movement with 70-hour power reserve, a slim 13.2mm case profile, and Seiko's distinctive dial texture, the SPB143 has been praised as one of the best mid-range dive watches available. It offers exceptional build quality and in-house movement technology at a fraction of Swiss luxury prices.

name: Presage SARB033
description: The Seiko SARB033 is widely regarded as one of the greatest value propositions in watchmaking. Featuring the reliable 6R15 automatic movement, a beautifully finished black dial, and solid build quality, the SARB033 punches well above its price point. Though discontinued, it remains a benchmark for affordable mechanical dress watches and continues to be highly sought after.

name: Snowflake
description: The Grand Seiko 'Snowflake' SBGA211 is one of the most celebrated watches in modern horology. Its textured white dial, inspired by the snow-covered landscape of the mountains near the Shinshu Watch Studio, is considered one of the most beautiful dials ever made. Powered by the Spring Drive calibre 9R65 — a unique movement combining mechanical and electronic technology — the Snowflake achieves ±1 second per day accuracy with a perfectly smooth sweeping seconds hand.

name: Carrera Chronograph
description: The TAG Heuer Carrera is one of the most legendary racing chronographs. Named after the Carrera Panamericana road race, it was designed by Jack Heuer in 1963 with a focus on legibility and elegance. The current generation features the Heuer 02 in-house automatic chronograph movement with an impressive 80-hour power reserve and column-wheel mechanism.

name: Portugieser Chronograph
description: The IWC Portugieser Chronograph is one of the most elegant chronographs in production. Originating from a 1939 request by two Portuguese merchants for a wristwatch with marine chronometer precision, the Portugieser has evolved into IWC's flagship dress chronograph. The current reference features the in-house calibre 69355 and the collection's signature railway-track minute ring and leaf-shaped hands.

name: Reverso Classic Medium Thin
description: The Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso is one of watchmaking's most distinctive and enduring designs. Created in 1931 to withstand the rigours of polo matches, the Reverso's reversible case is an Art Deco masterpiece of form meeting function. The Classic Medium Thin continues this legacy with refined proportions, a clean silver dial, and the elegant hand-wound calibre 657.

name: DW-5600E Classic
description: The Casio G-Shock DW-5600E is the spiritual successor to the original G-Shock DW-5000C from 1983. Its square case design has become one of the most iconic silhouettes in watchmaking. Virtually indestructible with 200m water resistance, shock resistance, and a module that can last over a decade on a single battery, the DW-5600E represents the pure essence of what G-Shock stands for.

name: Black Bay Fifty-Eight
description: The Tudor Black Bay Fifty-Eight is one of the most acclaimed dive watches of the modern era. Named after the year 1958 when Tudor's first dive watch was rated to 200m, the BB58 features a vintage-inspired 39mm case size, in-house MT5402 movement, and classic snowflake hands. Its proportions and wearability have made it a favourite among watch enthusiasts and a legitimate contender against watches costing many times its price.

name: Legend Diver
description: The Longines Legend Diver is a faithful re-edition of Longines' original 1960s dive watch. Featuring an internal rotating bezel operated by a second crown, this watch captures the golden age of skin diving in an elegant 42mm package. The current version is powered by the L888.5 automatic movement with silicon hairspring and 72-hour power reserve.

name: Seastar 1000 Powermatic 80
description: The Tissot Seastar 1000 Powermatic 80 is one of the best value dive watches available from a Swiss brand. Featuring the Powermatic 80 automatic movement with an impressive 80-hour power reserve, a unidirectional ceramic bezel, and 300m water resistance, it delivers genuine dive watch specifications at an accessible price.

name: Promaster Diver Eco-Drive
description: The Citizen Promaster Diver BN0151 is one of the most popular and affordable dive watches in the world. Powered by Citizen's Eco-Drive technology — converting any light into electrical energy — it never needs a battery change. With ISO 6425 dive watch certification, 200m water resistance, and robust build quality, the Promaster Diver represents exceptional value.

name: F-91W Classic Digital
description: The Casio F-91W is one of the best-selling watches of all time. Introduced in 1989, this simple, lightweight, and incredibly affordable digital watch has transcended its humble origins to become a cultural icon. Worn by world leaders, fashion icons, and billions of ordinary people, the F-91W embodies the democratic ideal of timekeeping — accurate, reliable time for everyone.

name: Luminor Marina
description: The Panerai Luminor Marina is the quintessential Panerai, featuring the brand's signature crown-protecting bridge device and cushion-shaped case. Originally developed for Italian Navy frogmen, the Luminor's distinctive 44mm case and highly legible sandwich dial make it instantly recognizable. The current reference features the in-house P.9010 automatic movement.

name: Overseas Automatic
description: The Vacheron Constantin Overseas is the luxury sports watch from the world's oldest continuously operating watch manufacturer. Featuring an integrated bracelet, interchangeable strap system (steel, leather, and rubber included), and the Geneva Seal-certified calibre 5100, the Overseas combines sporty versatility with haute horlogerie finishing.

specs.strapMaterial: Stainless steel bracelet (+ leather and rubber included)

name: Lange 1
description: The A. Lange & Söhne Lange 1 is one of the most important watch designs of the 20th century. Introduced in 1994 as part of the brand's rebirth after German reunification, the Lange 1 features an asymmetric dial layout with outsize date, power reserve indicator, and subsidiary seconds — all perfectly balanced despite being off-centre. The movement is hand-finished to the highest German standards with a three-quarter plate in untreated German silver.

name: Navitimer B01 Chronograph 43
description: The Breitling Navitimer is the iconic pilot's chronograph, first introduced in 1952 for the Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA). Its defining feature is the circular slide rule bezel, capable of performing all flight-related calculations including fuel consumption, rate of climb, and speed. The current B01 version features Breitling's in-house chronograph movement with column wheel and vertical coupling clutch.

name: Tangente 38
description: The NOMOS Tangente is the quintessential Bauhaus watch. Clean, minimal, and perfectly proportioned, the Tangente draws on the design legacy of the Bauhaus school in nearby Dessau. The reference 405 features the in-house Alpha calibre visible through the sapphire caseback, finished with the characteristic NOMOS Glashütte striping and blued screws.

name: Bambino Version 2
description: The Orient Bambino is widely considered the best entry-level dress watch in the world. Featuring an in-house automatic movement, a beautifully finished domed dial, and classic styling, the Bambino offers remarkable value. It has become the go-to recommendation for anyone seeking their first mechanical watch.

name: Big Bang Unico
description: The Hublot Big Bang Unico is the brand's flagship chronograph, embodying the 'Art of Fusion' philosophy. Featuring the in-house UNICO movement with flyback chronograph function, a column-wheel mechanism visible through the dial side, and Hublot's signature combination of materials, the Big Bang Unico represents bold, contemporary luxury watchmaking.

name: Chronomaster Original
description: The Zenith Chronomaster Original houses the legendary El Primero movement — the world's first automatic chronograph calibre, beating at the high frequency of 36,000 vph for 1/10th of a second precision. The current version features a tri-color dial paying homage to the original 1969 design, with a modern 38mm case size that perfectly frames this historically significant movement.

name: Octo Finissimo Automatic
description: The Bulgari Octo Finissimo has broken multiple world records for the thinnest watch in various categories. The Automatic version features the BVL 138 calibre at just 2.23mm thick, making the complete watch only 6.4mm thin. The Octo Finissimo's faceted case design, inspired by the octagonal dome of the Basilica of Maxentius in Rome, creates a striking interplay of light and shadow.

name: Khaki Field Mechanical
description: The Hamilton Khaki Field Mechanical is a direct descendant of Hamilton's military watches that served the US Armed Forces during World War II. This hand-wound version features the H-50 movement with an impressive 80-hour power reserve, a clean 38mm case, and the classic military dial with railroad-style minute track. It represents one of the best value Swiss mechanical watches available.

name: True Square Automatic
description: The Rado True Square showcases the brand's mastery of high-tech ceramic. The monobloc case is machined from a single block of high-tech ceramic, a material that is virtually scratch-proof, lightweight, and hypoallergenic. With its minimalist design and the automatic Powermatic 80.111 movement, the True Square represents Rado's philosophy of material innovation.

name: Classics Index Automatic
description: The Frederique Constant Classics Index Automatic embodies the brand's 'accessible luxury' philosophy. With a refined silver dial, blued steel hands, and the FC-303 automatic movement visible through a sapphire caseback, this dress watch delivers Swiss luxury aesthetics and in-house calibre technology at a fraction of traditional Swiss luxury prices.

name: Precisionist Chronograph
description: The Bulova Precisionist features a proprietary three-prong quartz crystal that vibrates at 262.144 kHz — eight times the frequency of standard quartz watches. This gives it accuracy to within seconds per year and a continuously sweeping second hand that mimics a mechanical watch. The Precisionist Chronograph adds a 1/1000th second chronograph, making it one of the most technologically advanced quartz watches available.

name: Altiplano Ultimate Automatic
description: The Piaget Altiplano has been the benchmark for ultra-thin elegant watchmaking since 1957. The Ultimate Automatic takes thinness to the extreme at just 4.30mm total thickness, housing the 910P calibre where the movement and case are integrated as one structure — the caseback serves as the mainplate. This represents the pinnacle of Piaget's ultra-thin expertise.

name: Pilot's Watch Chronograph 41
description: The IWC Pilot's Watch Chronograph continues a legacy that dates back to the 1930s when IWC first produced watches for military pilots. The reference IW377717 features the in-house 69385 calibre, a clean matte blue dial with luminous Arabic numerals, and the classic pilot's watch design language of ultimate legibility. The soft-iron inner case provides protection against magnetic fields.

name: Elegance Manual Wind
description: The Grand Seiko SBGW231 is a masterclass in understated refinement. Featuring the hand-wound calibre 9S64 with 72-hour power reserve, a pristine ivory dial with the Grand Seiko grammar of design, and a slim 11.6mm case, this watch demonstrates that true luxury lies in the details. The zaratsu-polished case creates mirror-like surfaces that shift between light and shadow.
